title: AE Wireless Transceiver
description: This is a ae wireless transceiver mod ,It can provide convenient wireless channel access.
body:
---

# AE Wireless Transceiver

***Please prioritize downloading the latest version.***

An addon for Applied Energistics 2 that introduces a fully wireless transceiver system to your AE2 network.

---

## Introduction

* User-friendly operation with an intuitive GUI.
* Completely wireless design — no cables, no power required.
* Full cross-dimensional transmission support.
* Customizable transmission channel names.
* Integrated FTB Teams support, ensuring complete isolation of channels and connections between different teams.
* Jade support for convenient in-world information display.

---

## What’s New in 1.3.0

* Added two new items:

  * **Wireless Connector**
  * **Wireless Destroyer**

Wireless Transceivers can now establish **direct connections to blocks without placing any cables**, making network setup significantly faster and more intuitive.

## New Items & Usage

### Wireless Connector

The Wireless Connector allows you to wirelessly bind and connect blocks to a transceiver channel.

**How to bind:**
Hold the Wireless Connector and **Shift + Right-click** a transceiver set to **Transmission Mode**.
This binds the connector to the transceiver’s current channel.

**How to connect:**
After binding, **Right-click** a target block to establish the wireless connection.

⚠️ **Important:**

1.Connections are **face-based**. Some blocks only support specific sides for connections, so make sure the binding and connection are performed on the correct face.

2.Starting from version 1.3.3-3, the connection defaults to full cache mode, which requires chunks to stay loaded; otherwise, the connection will be lost (can be modified in the configuration).



### Wireless Destroyer

The Wireless Destroyer allows you to safely remove existing wireless connections.

Simply **Right-click a connected block** to disconnect it from the channel.

---

## How to use Transceiver Block

When placed, the transceiver defaults to **Receiving Mode**.

To switch to **Transmission Mode**, click the toggle button in the GUI.

To select a channel, click the corresponding row in the channel list.
The selected row will be highlighted, indicating the active connection.

---

## Credits

The background and button textures used in this GUI are sourced from the ym-additions mod.
Special thanks to the development team for authorizing their use.

---
